name: RFISI: Mail Server Sending Mail to SPAM Servers
notes: This rule will notify when an internal mail server communicates with an IP that is known to send SPAM. Typically no legitimate mail server will be considered a SPAM server so this may indicate illicit activity or an internal infection. See https://ibm.biz/rfisi_threat_intel
conditions_text: when the context is Local to Remote
when a flow or an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Mail Servers, BB:HostReference: Mail Servers
when any of Destination IP are contained in any of Spam Senders - IP
TD_list: APPLY RFISI: Mail Server Sending Mail to SPAM Servers on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the context is Local to Remote
AND when a flow or an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Mail Servers, BB:HostReference: Mail Servers
AND when any of Destination IP are contained in any of Spam Senders - IP
RED_list: Event name: RFISI: Mail Server Sending Mail to SPAM Servers
Event description: An internal mail server is communicating with an IP that is considered to be sending SPAM.
